calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
TENTATIVE RULING: Hearing. Appearance required. No petition for final distribution or report of status of administration with a request for additional time has been filed yet, as required by Probate Code Section 12200.
Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk.
Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME.
Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall.
Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances.
For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879.
For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
EXAMINER: HELEN TROWBRIDGE

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lynn Searle for administrator Debra Dolch (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Approval of Report of Status of Administration.

The Court addressed interpretation of its prior order and noted concerns regarding the length of administration and the absence of an accounting despite the passage of more than two years since the date of death.

Ms. Searle requested additional time and reported that amendments to the inventories and appraisals were required due to issues arising from the sale of real property and questions regarding stock holdings. Ms. Searle stated that she had contacted the probate referee regarding certain shares originally listed and anticipated further amendment of the inventory and appraisal. Ms. Searle further reported that the accounting was being prepared but required resolution of these issues.

The Court noted that the BMW asset had not been properly addressed in the inventory and appraisal and must be corrected.

The Court indicated it would allow additional time to complete administration and preparation of a petition for final distribution and accounting. The Court stated that if a petition for final distribution and final accounting is filed, the matter may proceed on that basis; however, if not, an accounting covering approximately June 2023 through June 2025 would be required.

The Court took the petition under submission.

The Court set a further status hearing on close of estate for June 9, 2026, at 9:00 a.m., and indicated that the hearing would be vacated upon filing of a petition for final distribution.

The Court directed Ms. Searle to submit a proposed amended order.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
